2605.03723.title: Segmenting Human-LLM Co-authored Text via Change Point Detection
2605.03723.paper_summary: The rise of large language models (LLMs) has created an urgent need to distinguish between human-written and LLM-generated text to ensure authenticity and societal trust. Existing detectors typically provide a binary classification for an entire passage; however, this is insufficient for human--LLM co-authored text, where the objective is to localize specific segments authored by humans or LLMs. To bridge this gap, we propose algorithms to segment text into human- and LLM-authored pieces. Our key observation is that such a segmentation task is conceptually similar to classical change point detection in time-series analysis. Leveraging this analogy, we adapt change point detection to LLM-generated text detection, develop a weighted algorithm and a generalized algorithm to accommodate heterogeneous detection score variability, and establish the minimax optimality of our procedure. Empirically, we demonstrate the strong performance of our approach against a wide range of existing baselines.
2605.03723.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的興起使得區分人類撰寫的文本和LLM生成的文本成為一個迫切的需求，以確保真實性和社會信任。<lb/>現有的檢測器通常對整個段落提供二元分類；然而，這對於人類與LLM共同撰寫的文本來說是不足夠的，因為其目標是定位由人類或LLM撰寫的特定段落。<lb/>為了解決這一問題，我們提出了將文本分段為人類和LLM撰寫部分的算法。<lb/>我們的關鍵觀察是，這樣的分段任務在概念上類似於時間序列分析中的經典變更點檢測。<lb/>利用這一類比，我們將變更點檢測應用於LLM生成文本的檢測，開發了一種加權算法和一種通用算法，以適應異質檢測分數的變異性，並確立我們程序的最小最大最優性。<lb/>在實證方面，我們展示了我們的方法在各種現有基準上的強大表現。
2605.03723.author: Mengchu Li et.al.
2605.03723.authors: Mengchu Li, Jin Zhu, Jinglai Li, Chengchun Shi
